tools.name: create_race_plan
tools.description: Primary CourseProfiler tool for creating a personalized race plan and optional PDF. Use this after obtaining both a course input and runner input. Course input may be course_file, course.source with kind=url/artifact/raw_file, or course.crsprof_artifact_id. Do not use raw_json or reconstruct/synthes

tools.name: search_race_catalog
tools.description: Search CourseProfiler's race catalog by race/course name and return direct CRSProf URLs that can be used as course sources.

tools.name: import_course
tools.description: Import a fetchable official GPX/FIT/CRSProf URL, official ZIP containing exactly one GPX, uploaded source_file artifact, raw JSON payload, or inline/proxied course_file into a CRSProf artifact. Third-party route hosts such as Wikiloc may return 403 to server fetches; when a URL is blocked, ask the u

tools.name: enrich_course_waypoints
tools.description: Add structured aid stations, checkpoints, cutoffs, and canonical resources to a CRSProf artifact that lacks them. Before enriching, inspect whether the imported source already contains GPX/CRSProf waypoints; if it already contains GPX/CRSProf waypoints, avoid duplicate Start/Finish/aid stations and

tools.name: generate_course_segments
tools.description: Generate analytical course segments from an enriched CRSProf artifact after waypoint enrichment, or from a catalog CRSProf that already contains official waypoints/resources/cutoffs; then use the resulting artifact in create_race_plan for race planning, or call submit_course if and only if the user

tools.name: generate_runner_profile
tools.description: Generate a segment-evidence USRProf runner profile artifact from uploaded runner evidence or an existing .usrprof source. Before using this tool, ask what profile the user wants: target race/course, target distance/elevation range, general trail profile, or insights-only profile. Do not silently use

tools.name: submit_course
tools.description: Submit an official/trusted course for CourseProfiler catalog review. This is the only MCP tool that creates a catalog-review submission; import_course, enrich_course_waypoints, and generate_course_segments only create temporary artifacts. A final CRSProf artifact is not a submission. If there is no

tools.name: get_course_submission_requirements
tools.description: Use this before submit_course when the user gives only a race name or incomplete inputs. Returns the catalog-first course submission workflow, accepted route sources, metadata, waypoint enrichment, and upload requirements.

tools.name: get_race_plan_requirements
tools.description: Use this before create_race_plan when the user gives only a race name or incomplete inputs. Returns the required course and runner inputs and the next steps an assistant should take, including catalog matches when available.

tools.name: get_runner_profile_requirements
tools.description: Use this when runner input is missing or unclear. Explains how to obtain or generate runner input, what USRProf/evidence files should contain, whether GPX/FIT activities are enough, and how hosted MCP clients should upload artifacts.

tools.name: get_artifact_upload_requirements
tools.description: Use this when an assistant needs instructions for CourseProfiler's REST artifact upload flow or needs to explain why hosted MCP cannot upload bare local paths. This is an instruction helper, not a raw-byte MCP upload tool.

tools.name: get_job
tools.description: Poll a CourseProfiler async job by job_id.

tools.name: get_artifact
tools.description: Fetch artifact metadata and a private short-lived download URL by artifact_id.

tools.name: validate_course_profile
tools.description: Validate a CRSProf artifact or inline CRSProf JSON before race planning or catalog submission. This does not mutate artifacts or submit courses.

tools.name: validate_runner_profile
tools.description: Validate a USRProf artifact or inline USRProf JSON before creating personalized race plans. This does not mutate artifacts or generate a new profile.

tools.name: export_race_plan_pdf
tools.description: Export a Hi-Fi PDF from an existing CRSProf artifact. The CRSProf supplies the course/profile/plan data; options are presentation-only overrides such as title, subtitle, metric/imperial unit system, and file name. Omit unit_system unless the user explicitly asks for metric or imperial.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "create_race_plan" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Primary CourseProfiler tool for creating a personalized race plan and optional PDF. Use this after obtaining both a course input and runner input. Course input …

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "create_race_plan" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: Primary CourseProfiler tool for creating a personalized race plan and optional PDF. Use this after obtaining both a course input and runner input. Course input …

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "create_race_plan" is 4721 chars, far longer than this server's median (229). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: create_race_plan, search_race_catalog, generate_runner_profile, submit_course, get_artifact · Private data: create_race_plan, import_course, generate_runner_profile, submit_course, get_runner_profile_requirements, get_artifact, export_race_plan_pdf · Public sink: create_race_plan, import_course, generate_course_segments, generate_runner_profile, submit_course, get_course_submission_requirements, get_runner_profile_requirements, get_artifact_upload_requirements, validate_course_profile · Destructive: generate_runner_profile, submit_course.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: create_race_plan, search_race_catalog, generate_runner_profile, submit_course, get_artifact · Private data: create_race_plan, import_course, generate_runner_profile, submit_course, get_runner_profile_requirements, get_artifact, export_race_plan_pdf · Public sink: create_race_plan, import_course, generate_course_segments, generate_runner_profile, submit_course, get_course_submission_requirements, get_runner_profile_requirements, get_artifact_upload_requirements, validate_course_profile · Destructive: generate_runner_profile, submit_course

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
